title: Real Estate Giants Compass and Zillow Fight Over the Future of the Market
description: Both companies face lawsuits and antitrust inquiries as they attempt to control the experience of home buying.
author: Elizabeth A. Harris

title: Flipping a Philip Johnson: The Booth House Gets a Lifeline
description: In Bedford, N.Y., an architect and builder duo are betting that the Glass House architect’s first residential commission can be saved — and put back on the market.
author: Daniela Gorny and Katherine Marks

title: Can I Trust the ‘Zestimate’ for My Home on Zillow?
description: According to a real estate attorney, the estimate “is only as good as the data that is fed into the system.”
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: $2.6 Million Homes in Sicily
description: A stone house with views of Mount Etna, an 1893 villa with seven bedrooms, and a seaside house in six-bathroom house in Porto Ulisse.
author: Lana Bortolot

title: Homes for Sale in Manhattan and Queens
description: This week’s properties are in Greenwich Village, on the Upper East Side and in Hunters Point.
author: Heather Senison

title: Homes for Sale in New York and Connecticut
description: This week’s properties are a four-bedroom house in Pleasantville, N.Y., and a five-bedroom house in Stamford, Conn.
author: Anne Mancuso and Alicia Napierkowski

title: More People Live, Work and Play All in the Same Building
description: A study tracked 10 years of new development combining retail, office space and residences.
author: Arnesa A. Howell

title: In Manhattan, Learning to Not Always Take ‘Yes’ for an Answer
description: A woman wanted to move to a one-bedroom, and she liked the Upper East Side. But after negotiating and signing a lease, something didn’t seem right.
author: D.W. Gibson and Katherine Marks

title: The Growing Movement for Shelters to Make Room for Pets
description: Shelters recognize that many would rather sleep on the street than leave their animals behind.
author: Elizabeth A. Harris

title: How to Make Wallpaper Art
description: Start out with a low-stakes installation on an art canvas. You’ll learn the basics, and you can easily undo it.
author: Tim Heffernan and Clark Hodgin

title: A.I. Boom Leads to Record Home Prices in San Francisco
description: The Bay Area boasts the highest median home prices in the country and experienced the sharpest annual price increases.
author: Kami Rieck

title: How Do I Stop a Bad Landlord From Ruining My Neighborhood?
description: A well-organized group of neighbors is often the most powerful force.
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: $1.3 Million Homes in Sweden
description: A 10-bedroom manor from 1806, a 25-acre farm estate near the Baltic Sea, and a five-bedroom modern farmhouse.
author: Roxana Popescu

title: Homes for Sale in New York and New Jersey
description: This week’s properties are a four-bedroom in Laurel, N.Y., and a five-bedroom in Bridgewater, N.J.
author: Claudia Gryvatz Copquin and Jill P. Capuzzo

title: Homes for Sale in Brooklyn and Manhattan
description: This week’s properties are in Williamsburg, on the Upper West Side and in Hudson Heights.
author: Heather Senison

title: Still Mailing Your Rent? You’re Hardly Alone.
description: Those who pay their rent online are now the majority — but just barely.
author: Joyce Cohen

title: A Striking Costa Rican Beach House Pays Tribute to Latin America
description: A builder and an architect collaborated on a family beach compound using local materials and with a patio and a pool at the center.
author: Tim McKeough
